mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and the consumer price index indicates strong demand outpacing supply, which boosts corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL rises faster than RSAFS, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a monthly increase in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when global commodity prices spike simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of observations, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10 in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power when real yields decline concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a robust labor market expansion that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, which drags on economic growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal is overwhelmed by concurrent fiscal stimulus announcements.
